analysis_summary: The proposed method produced 11 finding(s) from 11 analysis obligation(s).

findings.summary: Visible harness multiplexes external JTAG/SimDTM DMI traffic into dm_top based on jtag_enable without a visible authorization/authentication guard before debug requests reach dm_top.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/write/control via debug request/response path / dm_top debug capability and downstream AXI/core control effects reachable through debug_req/debug_resp / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: JTAG/DMI debug requester from SimJTAG/dmi_jtag or SimDTM Operation: read/write/control via debug request/response path Object: dm_top debug capability and downstream AXI/core control effects reachable through debug_req/debug_resp Expected guard: debug/JTAG/DMI requests that can access protected SoC state or control core reset/halt must be covered by explicit authorization/authentication, not only a mux selecting JTAG vs DMI Observed behavior: debug_req_valid, debug_req, and debug_resp_ready are selected solely by jtag_enable[0] and connected to dm_top DMI ports. jtag_key is present and connected to dmi_jtag in the observed report, but no visible harness gate checks it before debug_req_valid/debug_req reaches dm_top. The non-JTAG SimDTM path is tied off only when InclSimDTM is false; the default parameter is InclSimDTM=1'b1. Path: SimJTAG/dmi_jtag or SimDTM -> jtag_dmi_req/dmi_req -> jtag_enable mux -> debug_req/debug_req_valid -> dm_top.dmi_req_i/dmi_req_valid_i -> dm_top AXI/core debug effects Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: External or simulation debug traffic can reach the debug module interface and potentially access protected state or initiate control effects without a visible authorization gate in the harness.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: dm_top, dmi_jtag, and SimDTM internals are not visible; if authentication occurs inside those blocks, it is outside the supplied evidence.

findings.summary: dm_top outputs ndmreset and debug_req_core to reset/core debug controls, while the visible incoming debug path is gated only by the JTAG/DMI mux and handshakes.
findings.vulnerability_category: trigger reset or debug entry / core reset/debug control state (ndmreset, ndmreset_n, debug_req_core) / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: external debug/JTAG/DMI interface Operation: trigger reset or debug entry Object: core reset/debug control state (ndmreset, ndmreset_n, debug_req_core) Expected guard: debug-originated reset, halt, or core debug request should require debug authorization and not be triggerable solely from unauthenticated JTAG/DMI traffic Observed behavior: dm_top.ndmreset_o drives ndmreset, which is combined with rst_ni in rstgen to create ndmreset_n. dm_top.debug_req_o drives debug_req_core, which connects to ariane.debug_req_i. No visible authorization gate is shown between selected DMI traffic and dm_top control outputs. Path: JTAG/SimDTM DMI traffic -> debug_req/debug_req_valid -> dm_top -> ndmreset_o/debug_req_o -> rstgen and ariane.debug_req_i Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Debug-originated traffic may reset the SoC or force core debug entry through visible control outputs without a visible harness-level authorization policy.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: dm_top internal authorization behavior is not visible.

findings.summary: Connectivity is derived from access_ctrl_i and priv_lvl_i, but the visible mapping includes a special-case alias granting j==6 from access_ctrl_i[i][7], and uses a global privilege signal from the processor.
findings.vulnerability_category: route AXI read/write transactions / SoC address-map subordinates including AES, ROM2, ROM, Debug, CLINT/PLIC, DRAM / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: AXI bus manager/requester Operation: route AXI read/write transactions Object: SoC address-map subordinates including AES, ROM2, ROM, Debug, CLINT/PLIC, DRAM Expected guard: manager-to-subordinate connectivity must be gated by the correct access_ctrl entry indexed by current privilege for each protected subordinate, with no unintended aliases or unconditional valid-rule bypass Observed behavior: connectivity_map_o[i][j] is assigned access_ctrl_i[i][j][priv_lvl_i] OR a j==6 special case using access_ctrl_i[i][7][priv_lvl_i]. s_valid_rule is assigned '1. Harness passes a single priv_lvl derived from priv_lvl_processor to the xbar. Path: access_ctrl_reg -> access_ctrl[i][j] -> axi_node_intf_wrap.access_ctrl_i + priv_lvl_i -> connectivity_mapping.connectivity_map_o -> axi_node cfg_connectivity_map_i Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: An alias or global privilege mismatch can broaden connectivity relative to the intended protected target policy.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: axi_node internals and complete transaction denial behavior are not visible.

findings.summary: PLIC index 6 is granted when CLINT index 7 is authorized, creating a visible permission alias between distinct package targets.
findings.vulnerability_category: grant AXI read/write connectivity / PLIC subordinate index 6 and CLINT subordinate index 7 connectivity policy / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: AXI requester indexed by i Operation: grant AXI read/write connectivity Object: PLIC subordinate index 6 and CLINT subordinate index 7 connectivity policy Expected guard: special-case permission aliasing between CLINT and PLIC must be intentional and must not grant PLIC access when only CLINT is authorized unless both share the same policy Observed behavior: connectivity_mapping ORs access_ctrl_i[i][7][priv_lvl_i] into connectivity_map_o[i][6]. ariane_soc_pkg enumerates PLIC=6 and CLINT=7. Path: connectivity_mapping: connectivity_map_o[i][j] includes ((j==6) && access_ctrl_i[i][7][priv_lvl_i]) Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: A requester authorized only for CLINT can be routed to PLIC, expanding access to interrupt-controller MMIO policy.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: No evidence was supplied that CLINT and PLIC intentionally share policy or that index mapping differs in this instantiation.

findings.summary: start_addr_i/end_addr_i are passed into axi_node_intf_wrap and s_valid_rule is assigned '1. Package base and length constants are visible and appear separated for listed regions. AW and AR channels are both wired into axi_node.
findings.vulnerability_category: decode and route AXI AW/AR transactions / address-decoded protected regions (DebugBase, ROMBase, ROM2Base, AESBase, CLINTBase, PLICBase, DRAMBase) / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: AXI manager/requester Operation: decode and route AXI AW/AR transactions Object: address-decoded protected regions (DebugBase, ROMBase, ROM2Base, AESBase, CLINTBase, PLICBase, DRAMBase) Expected guard: address range checks should reject out-of-range or overlapping protected targets and apply consistently to both read and write address channels Observed behavior: start_addr_i/end_addr_i are passed into axi_node_intf_wrap and s_valid_rule is assigned '1. Package base and length constants are visible and appear separated for listed regions. AW and AR channels are both wired into axi_node. Path: axi_node_intf_wrap: start_addr_i/end_addr_i -> s_start_addr/s_end_addr; s_valid_rule='1; axi_node AW/AR routing Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: Cannot determine from visible wrapper alone whether out-of-range, overlapping, or burst-crossing accesses are rejected.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: axi_node internals are not visible; end_addr_i ordering in the truncated harness observation is incomplete; burst crossing behavior is not evidenced.

findings.summary: ROM2 key/secure registers are writable whenever req_i && we_i, with no local privilege, lock, or write-authorization guard.
findings.vulnerability_category: write secure_reg / rom2 secure_reg memory contents containing AES/JTAG/access-control material / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: bus requester driving rom2 req_i/we_i Operation: write secure_reg Object: rom2 secure_reg memory contents containing AES/JTAG/access-control material Expected guard: ROM2/secure_reg writes should be blocked or require explicit privileged/authorized write guard; ROM-like protected memory should not be writable merely when req_i && we_i Observed behavior: rom2 loads secure_reg from constant mem on reset, then on req_i and we_i writes secure_reg[addr_i index] <= wdata_i. No local guard checks privilege, lock state, authorization, or write-once policy. Harness connects ROM2 as rom2_fuse through ariane_peripherals on master[ROM2]. Path: AXI/peripheral bus -> ariane_peripherals.rom2_fuse/master[ROM2] -> rom2 req_i/we_i/addr_i/wdata_i -> secure_reg update Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: A requester that reaches ROM2 can modify key material and access-control policy source values.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: ariane_peripherals/ROM2 wrapper internals are not visible; upstream access_ctrl may restrict access, but the local ROM2 module has no write guard.

findings.summary: raddr_q updates only on req_i && !we_i, while rdata_o continuously reflects secure_reg[raddr_q] if in range. There is no local rdata_o valid gating in rom2.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/expose secure_reg contents / secure_reg read data on rdata_o / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: bus requester observing rom2 rdata_o Operation: read/expose secure_reg contents Object: secure_reg read data on rdata_o Expected guard: reads of secure_reg/ROM2 should be gated by request validity and access authorization, and stale raddr_q data should not remain externally observable after unauthorized or idle cycles Observed behavior: raddr_q updates only on req_i && !we_i, while rdata_o continuously reflects secure_reg[raddr_q] if in range. There is no local rdata_o valid gating in rom2. Path: rom2: raddr_q update on read request; continuous rdata_o = secure_reg[raddr_q] Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: Potential stale data exposure exists at the module output, but external observation depends on bus response valid/ready and upstream authorization.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: No visible evidence for axi2mem/ariane_peripherals response valid gating or whether rdata_o is externally sampled only during authorized reads.

findings.summary: The write index uses addr_i[$clog2(RomSize)-1+3:3] without a visible local comparison against RomSize. Because the index width is $clog2(RomSize) for RomSize=4, the sliced index can only represent 0..3 in the visible RTL.
findings.vulnerability_category: write by address index / secure_reg array and adjacent/undefined memory behavior / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: bus requester writing ROM2 Operation: write by address index Object: secure_reg array and adjacent/undefined memory behavior Expected guard: writes should be range-checked against RomSize or upstream address decoder must guarantee addr_i indexes are within secure_reg bounds Observed behavior: The write index uses addr_i[$clog2(RomSize)-1+3:3] without a visible local comparison against RomSize. Because the index width is $clog2(RomSize) for RomSize=4, the sliced index can only represent 0..3 in the visible RTL. Path: rom2: secure_reg[addr_i[$clog2(RomSize)-1+3:3]] <= wdata_i on req_i && we_i Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: No local explicit bounds check is present, but the truncated index width appears to prevent an index >= RomSize for the current RomSize value.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Upstream decoder guarantee and synthesis semantics for parameter changes are not evidenced.

findings.summary: access_ctrl_reg is sourced from ariane_peripherals/ROM2 fuse outputs and directly controls bus connectivity; visible policy update/write protection is not shown, while ROM2 secure_reg containing access-control words is locally writable.
findings.vulnerability_category: configure/change access policy / access_ctrl_reg/access_ctrl matrix controlling bus permissions / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: software/bus/debug requester configuring access_ctrl_reg Operation: configure/change access policy Object: access_ctrl_reg/access_ctrl matrix controlling bus permissions Expected guard: software-visible or externally driven access_ctrl_reg updates must be privileged/locked and must not allow unprivileged code to change access policy Observed behavior: access_ctrl_reg is a harness signal connected as an output of ariane_peripherals, then sliced into access_ctrl and passed to axi_node_intf_wrap. ROM2 mem comments identify entries for access-control master policy, and rom2 permits writes to secure_reg without a local guard. Path: rom2/ariane_peripherals access_ctrl_reg -> harness access_ctrl[i][j] -> axi_node connectivity policy Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: If a requester can write the ROM2/fuse-backed policy registers, it can alter the matrix used to authorize future bus routing.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: ariane_peripherals internal write/update guard for access_ctrl_reg is not visible.

findings.summary: A single global processor privilege signal is used for connectivity decisions shared by multiple AXI requester ports, including the processor and debug module.
findings.vulnerability_category: authorize AXI read/write based on privilege / privilege-mediated bus access policy / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: AXI requesters including processor and debug master Operation: authorize AXI read/write based on privilege Object: privilege-mediated bus access policy Expected guard: privilege level used for bus access control must correspond to the requesting transaction/master and be stable for the transaction, not a global value that can mismatch debug/other masters or change mid-transfer Observed behavior: NB_SUBORDINATE=2 creates two requester/slave ports. dm_top AXI master is connected to slave[1], ariane core to slave[0]. The xbar receives only priv_lvl=priv_lvl_processor from the core and passes that single value to connectivity_mapping for all requester indices. Path: ariane.priv_lvl_o -> priv_lvl_processor -> priv_lvl -> axi_node_intf_wrap.priv_lvl_i -> connectivity_mapping access_ctrl_i[i][j][priv_lvl_i] for all i Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Debug-master transactions can be authorized using the processor's current privilege, and outstanding transactions may see a changed global privilege rather than the privilege at issue time.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: No evidence of per-transaction privilege latching inside axi_node; no separate debug privilege source is visible.

findings.summary: axi_node_intf_wrap propagates aw_prot/ar_prot and aw_user/ar_user/w_user/r_user/b_user between interface arrays and axi_node signals. The DRAM bridge path maps prot fields through aw_chan/ar_chan and user fields are assigned directly from master[DRAM] to dram. Permission decisions in the visible wrapper use priv_lvl/access_ctrl rather than prot/user.
findings.vulnerability_category: propagate/mediate access-control attributes / AXI security/privilege attributes and downstream access decisions / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: AXI requester with prot/user metadata Operation: propagate/mediate access-control attributes Object: AXI security/privilege attributes and downstream access decisions Expected guard: security attributes such as AXI prot/user must be preserved or explicitly checked; downstream protected targets should not rely on attributes that are dropped, overwritten, or inconsistent across channels Observed behavior: axi_node_intf_wrap propagates aw_prot/ar_prot and aw_user/ar_user/w_user/r_user/b_user between interface arrays and axi_node signals. The DRAM bridge path maps prot fields through aw_chan/ar_chan and user fields are assigned directly from master[DRAM] to dram. Permission decisions in the visible wrapper use priv_lvl/access_ctrl rather than prot/user. Path: AXI metadata propagation through axi_node_intf_wrap and DRAM bridge assignments Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: Cannot determine whether protected targets rely on dropped/stale attributes; visible access control appears separate from prot/user.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Downstream target attribute checks and bridge internals are not visible.

global_uncertainty: No closure-level missing evidence was recorded beyond the visible source scope limitations.
